rent.note: furnished 1BR in Tay Ho or Ba Dinh nomad area; $365-525 range

utilities.note: electric + water + fiber internet; hot summers mean higher A/C bills

groceries.note: local wet markets; fresh produce very cheap

diningOut.note: street pho $1-2, mid-range $3-8; mix of local + expat cafes

transit.note: Grab bike/car; 5km ride ~$1.70; monthly Grab budget

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; growing coworking scene in Tay Ho area

misc.note: gym, entertainment, personal care, cultural activities, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers applied to RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Raise for peak tourist/weather months, lower for off-peak.

evidence.claim: Hanoi 1BR apartment average $399/month; modern serviced apt $440-525
evidence.quote: Average rent is $399.00/month for a 1-bedroom apartment. 10-12 million dong ($440–$525) will get you a spacious and modern serviced one-bedroom apartment in a prime location
Plan for approximately $944/month as a digital nomad in Hanoi, covering coworking, accommodation, food, and local transport

evidence.claim: Grab 5km ride $1.70; Big Mac $3.20; Starbucks latte $1.90
evidence.quote: A Big Mac costs $3.20 and a Starbucks latte goes for $1.90 in Hanoi. A 5km Uber ride costs $1.70
Rent for a one-bedroom apartment in Tay Ho can range from 5-10 million VND (approximately $220-$440 USD) per month
